title: Block duplicate recovery response when opening answered push notification
description:
Opening a recovery-request push notification from the OS can navigate to a recovery request the user already responded to. The screen still allows submitting a response again.

Add handling similar to the other push/deep-link guard: detect already-responded state (or stale notification) and show an informative dialog instead of the full respond flow, so stewards cannot accidentally double-submit.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping an OS notification for a recovery request the user already responded to shows a clear dialog (no second submit path). Fresh unanswered requests behave unchanged.

title: Merge PR via gh api when gh pr merge fails (multi-worktree main)
description:
When another git worktree already has main checked out, `gh pr merge` can fail with: fatal: 'main' is already used by worktree at ... Merge on GitHub without touching local refs:

```bash
gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>/merge -X PUT \\
  -f merge_method=squash \\
  -f commit_title='Short title (#123)'
```

Use merge_method merge or rebase if the repo prefers. Optionally delete the remote branch afterward via the GitHub UI or refs API.
notes: Observed 2026-05-01 merging mplorentz/horcrux PR121 from horcrux_wt_pr121 while main lived in horcrux_app worktree.

title: Agent playbook: clean bead labels/metadata when closing PR-linked work
description:
Use this checklist whenever you close a bead that tracked GitHub work (branch / worktree / PR). Goal: no stale workflow labels or obsolete paths on closed issues.

## When this applies
- Bead has (or had) metadata like `branch`, `worktree`, or maps to a PR.
- You are closing after merge, or abandoning with a documented reason.

## Close the bead
- `bd close <id> -r "..."` with a concrete reason (PR URL or plain-language outcome).

## Right after close — Beads hygiene (required)
See also `.beads/STAGES.md` (remove old `stage:*` / `blocked-on:*` when workflow moves).

1. **Strip stale workflow labels** on the closed issue so it does not look "in progress":
   - Remove `blocked-on:agent`, `blocked-on:ci`, `blocked-on:human` if no longer true.
   - Remove active `stage:*` labels (`stage:agent-fixing`, `stage:pr-open`, `stage:implementing`, etc.). Closed beads should not carry labels that imply ongoing work.

2. **Post-merge cleanup label**: If you temporarily use `stage:cleanup-needed` for worktree/branch deletion, **remove it once cleanup is done** — do not leave it on a closed bead.

3. **Metadata** — keep what is still true, clear what is not:
   - Set `pr_url` to the full PR URL when known: `bd update <id> --set-metadata pr_url=https://github.com/.../pull/N`
   - After you delete the local branch and remove the git worktree, clear branch/worktree metadata: `bd update <id> --set-metadata branch= --set-metadata worktree=`

4. **Notes** — append one line of factual cleanup for future you: `bd update <id> --append-notes "..."` (e.g. worktree path removed, local branch deleted).

5. **Optional comment** — if verification matters, `bd comment <id> "..."` with CI commands or evidence.

## Same session — Git hygiene (often paired)
- Remove dedicated worktrees when the branch is merged or abandoned: `git worktree remove <path>` then `git worktree prune`.
- Delete merged local branches: `git branch -d <branch>`.
- In app repos, `flutter clean` in that worktree if you are retiring it.

Agents should treat steps under "Right after close" as part of closing the loop, not optional polish.
